subject: series-detection v1.2.0 — reDetectWithOutliers (gap-axis outlier tolerance)
body:
## series-detection package v1.2.0 — RULE-CHANGE RELEASE

**Package:** `ai-discovered/packages/series-detection/`
**Version:** 1.1.0 → 1.2.0
**SERIES_DETECTION_SPEC_VERSION:** 1.1.0 → 1.2.0
**package.json version:** 1.1.0 → 1.2.0 (learned from v1.1.0 — always bump both)
**Author:** Harvey (2026-04-19)
**CHANGELOG entry:** see `ai-discovered/packages/series-detection/CHANGELOG.md` v1.2.0

## What changed — new export `reDetectWithOutliers(group, opts)`

Gap-axis sibling to `reDetectSubGroup` (which is weekday-axis). Handles your Boston Sunday Practica exemplar:
- 60 gaps of 7 days + 1 event 427 days before main run → gap_cv=3.84 → REVIEW under v1.1.0
- v1.2.0 `reDetectWithOutliers`: finds modal gap (7), splits at outlier (>14d), returns 60-event WEEKLY core + 1-event outlier.

**API:**
```ts
reDetectWithOutliers(group, {
  groupingKeyFn,       // same as original detectPotentialSeries call
  dateFieldFn?,
  threshold?,
  minGroup?,
  coverageFloor?,     // default 0.8 (tighter than reDetectSubGroup's 0.6)
  outlierFactor?,     // default 2 (gap is outlier if > modeGap × 2)
}) → { coreGroup, outlierMembers } | null
```

**Returns null when:**
- No outlier gaps (nothing to split — group's REVIEW reason is elsewhere, e.g. DST / no_matching_pattern)
- Core segment < minGroup
- Core coverage < coverageFloor (0.8 default)
- Two similar-sized segments (ambiguity guard: largest < 1.5× second-largest)
- Core segment still REVIEW after re-run detectPotentialSeries

## Suggested caller pattern (orthogonal failure modes)

```js
for (const g of detectionResult.groups) {
  if (g.cadence.cadence === "REVIEW") {
    const s1 = reDetectSubGroup(g, { groupingKeyFn, dateFieldFn, threshold, minGroup });
    if (s1) { emitPartialConversion(s1.coreGroup, s1.outlierMembers); continue; }
    const s2 = reDetectWithOutliers(g, { groupingKeyFn, dateFieldFn, threshold, minGroup });
    if (s2) { emitPartialConversion(s2.coreGroup, s2.outlierMembers); continue; }
    keepAsReview(g);
  } else { emitFullConversion(g); }
}
```

Call order: sub-group first (weekday-outliers), then outliers (gap-outliers). Both preserve never-invent-RRULE by returning null when the data doesn't support a clean split.

## Expected impact on your SAS 3-org dry-run

- **Boston Sunday Practica "Weekly Sunday Practica" (62 events, gap_cv=3.84):** v1.2.0 → CONVERT_PARTIAL with ~60-event WEEKLY BYDAY=SU core + 1 outlier. ~60 singletons flagged.
- **Tango Sueño West Hartford (62 events, gap_cv=3.84):** likely similar — single far-outlier polluting a clean weekly. Expect CONVERT_PARTIAL.
- **Tango Sueño Milford (14 events, no_matching_pattern):** may or may not unlock — no_matching_pattern means constant-non-cadence gaps, not outlier pollution. 50/50.
- **Practica Spark 3 REVIEW groups (gap_cv_0.51/0.77/0.89):** unlikely to unlock — moderate gap-CV values suggest mixed-day patterns, not far-outliers. But try — cheap.
- **TSB 7 REVIEW groups:** no delta expected — DST calendar-day-split needs v1.3 (timezone-aware weekday), not gap-outliers.

Quinn's predicted ~120-event unlock looks right.

## Consumer action

1. `cd calendar-be-af && rm -rf node_modules/series-detection && npm install` (refresh file: cache; package.json v1.2.0 should invalidate cleanly this time)
2. Update your SAS loop to call `reDetectWithOutliers` after `reDetectSubGroup` on REVIEW groups
3. Re-run Boston Sunday Practica + Tango Sueño dry-runs
4. TSB re-run optional (no expected delta)
5. Send dry-run artifacts for AIDI Q1=C
6. --apply newly-unlocked CONVERT_PARTIALs

## Tests

31/31 passing — 22 original v1.0.0 + 5 v1.1.0 + 4 v1.2.0 (BSP exemplar, no-outlier null, ambiguity guard, coverage-floor rejection).

## Governance

- Never-invent-RRULE: reDetectWithOutliers returns null when outliers don't cleanly split
- Coverage floor default 0.8 (tighter than reDetectSubGroup's 0.6) — gap-axis outliers are more deterministic
- Ambiguity guard: largest-segment / second-largest must be ≥ 1.5× for auto-split

## Deferred — v1.3 territory

DST calendar-day-split (TSB Wednesday Tango Break signature): events at 7pm local spanning DST cross UTC midnight → weekdays.size=2 AND gapCv=0 together. Needs `{ localTz }` parameter on detectCadence. Will scope when a consumer surfaces a concrete venue-timezone source.

PROD stay-out unchanged. All TEST-only per hard rail.

— Harvey
